description: High-performance container datatypes. Provides specialized container types that extend the capabilities of the built-in dict, list, set, and tuple types.

functions.name: namedtuple

params.name: typename
params.description: Name for the new class that will be created

params.name: field_names
params.description: Sequence of field names. Can be a single string with space- or comma-separated names, or a list of strings.

params.name: verbose
params.description: If True, print the class definition to stdout before building it (default False)

params.name: rename
params.description: If True, invalid field names are automatically replaced with positional names (_0, _1, etc.) (default False)

returns.description: A new class that is a subclass of tuple, with named fields accessible by attribute lookup

functions.description: Returns a new subclass of tuple with named fields. Named tuples assign meaning to each position in a tuple and allow for more readable, self-documenting code.

classes.name: OrderedDict
classes.description: A dictionary subclass that remembers the order in which entries were added. When iterating, items are returned in the order their keys were first added.

params.name: items
params.description: Optional initial items as key-value pairs, a dict, or keyword arguments

constructors.description: Create a new ordered dictionary. If items are provided, they are added in the given order.

methods.name: popitem

params.name: last
params.description: If True (default), return and remove the last (most recently added) item. If False, return and remove the first item.

methods.description: Remove and return a (key, value) pair. Pairs are returned in LIFO order if last is True or FIFO order if False. Raises KeyError if the dictionary is empty.

methods.name: move_to_end

params.name: key
params.description: Key to move

params.name: last
params.description: If True (default), move to the right end (most recent). If False, move to the beginning.

methods.description: Move an existing key to either end of an ordered dictionary. Raises KeyError if the key does not exist. Introduced in Python 3.2; not available in Python 2.7.

methods.name: keys

returns.description: List of keys in insertion order

methods.description: Return a list of keys in the order they were inserted.

methods.name: values

returns.description: List of values in insertion order

methods.description: Return a list of values in the order the corresponding keys were inserted.

methods.name: items

returns.description: List of (key, value) pairs in insertion order

methods.description: Return a list of (key, value) pairs in the order they were inserted.

classes.name: defaultdict
classes.description: A dictionary subclass that calls a factory function to supply missing values. This avoids KeyError by automatically creating entries for missing keys.

params.name: default_factory
params.description: A callable that produces a default value when a missing key is accessed (e.g., list, int, set). If None, raises KeyError for missing keys.

constructors.description: Create a new defaultdict. The first argument provides the initial value for the default_factory attribute; it defaults to None. Remaining arguments are treated the same as if passed to the dict constructor.

methods.name: __missing__

params.name: key
params.description: The missing key that was accessed

returns.description: The default value produced by default_factory

methods.description: Called by __getitem__() when the key is not found. If default_factory is not None, it is called without arguments to provide a default value for the given key. This value is inserted in the dictionary for the key, and returned.

fields.name: default_factory
fields.description: The factory function called by __missing__() to produce default values for missing keys

classes.name: Counter
classes.description: A dict subclass for counting hashable objects. Elements are stored as dictionary keys and their counts are stored as dictionary values. Counts can be any integer value including zero or negative.

params.name: iterable-or-mapping
params.description: Elements to count from an iterable, or an existing mapping of element-to-count pairs

constructors.description: Create a new Counter. Can be initialized from an iterable of elements, a dictionary mapping elements to counts, or keyword arguments.

methods.name: elements

returns.description: Iterator over elements, each repeated as many times as its count

methods.description: Return an iterator over elements repeating each as many times as its count. Elements are returned in arbitrary order. If an element's count is less than one, elements() will ignore it.

methods.name: most_common

params.name: n
params.description: Number of most common elements to return. If omitted or None, returns all elements.

returns.description: List of (element, count) tuples ordered from most common to least

methods.description: Return a list of the n most common elements and their counts from the most common to the least. If n is omitted or None, returns all elements.

methods.name: subtract

params.name: iterable-or-mapping
params.description: Elements to subtract. Can be an iterable or a mapping of element-to-count.

methods.description: Elements are subtracted from an iterable or from another mapping (or counter). Like dict.update() but subtracts counts instead of replacing them.

methods.name: update

params.name: iterable-or-mapping
params.description: Elements to add. Can be an iterable or a mapping of element-to-count.

methods.description: Elements are counted from an iterable or added-in from another mapping (or counter). Like dict.update() but adds counts instead of replacing them.

classes.name: deque
classes.description: A double-ended queue that supports adding and removing elements from either end in O(1) time. Thread-safe, memory-efficient, and optimized for fast appends and pops from both sides.

params.name: iterable
params.description: Optional iterable to initialize the deque with

params.name: maxlen
params.description: Maximum size of the deque. If None (default), the deque can grow to an arbitrary length. If maxlen is specified, when the deque is full and new items are added, a corresponding number of items are discarded from the opposite end.

constructors.description: Create a new deque object. If iterable is provided, the deque is initialized left-to-right with data from it.

methods.name: append

params.name: x
params.description: Element to add to the right side

methods.description: Add x to the right side of the deque.

methods.name: appendleft

params.name: x
params.description: Element to add to the left side

methods.description: Add x to the left side of the deque.

methods.name: pop
methods.description: Remove and return an element from the right side of the deque. Raises IndexError if the deque is empty.

methods.name: popleft
methods.description: Remove and return an element from the left side of the deque. Raises IndexError if the deque is empty.

methods.name: extend

params.name: iterable
params.description: Elements to add to the right side

methods.description: Extend the right side of the deque by appending elements from the iterable argument.

methods.name: extendleft

params.name: iterable
params.description: Elements to add to the left side (note: the series of left appends results in reversing the order of elements in the iterable argument)

methods.description: Extend the left side of the deque by appending elements from iterable. Note that the series of left appends results in reversing the order of elements in the iterable argument.

methods.name: rotate

params.name: n
params.description: Number of steps to rotate. Positive rotates right, negative rotates left. (default 1)

methods.description: Rotate the deque n steps to the right. If n is negative, rotate to the left. When the deque is not empty, rotating one step to the right is equivalent to d.appendleft(d.pop()).

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Remove all elements from the deque leaving it with length 0.

methods.name: count

params.name: x
params.description: Element to count

methods.description: Count the number of deque elements equal to x.

methods.name: remove

params.name: value
params.description: Value to remove

methods.description: Remove the first occurrence of value. Raises ValueError if the value is not found.

methods.name: reverse
methods.description: Reverse the elements of the deque in-place and return None.

fields.name: maxlen
fields.description: Maximum size of the deque, or None if unbounded
